1:00 PM - Lunch & Rest: She eats with th: 1:00 PM - Lunch & Rest: She eats with the community. She learns about their food, agriculture, and seasonal cycles.

6:00 PM - Analysis: Back at her makeshif: 6:00 PM - Analysis: Back at her makeshift office, she transcribes interviews and organizes field notes. She cross-references with historical records.
8:00 PM - Reflection: She writes in her: 8:00 PM - Reflection: She writes in her research journal, reflecting on what she learned and what questions emerged.

The hard truths of CRF service.
Risk to Life: Counter-terrorism and bord: Risk to Life: Counter-terrorism and border operations carry significant risk. Casualties do occur. You must be prepared for the ultimate sacrifice.

Strict Discipline: Military-style discip: Strict Discipline: Military-style discipline is non-negotiable. Disobedience can result in court-martial.
Family Separation: Extended deployments: Family Separation: Extended deployments mean months away from family. Anniversaries and children
